category_title: Relationship-dependent students sensitive to teacher attitude
scenario_title: Asking for help with a graphing problem
minimal_context: John is working on a math assignment and asks the tutor for help with a graphing problem.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Here is your math task. You need to graph that problem.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: You gotta like, graph that problem with there.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: You're just telling me what I need to do, not how to do it.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: You just need to complete the work.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: [voice sounds angry] You aren't really teaching me anything useful! I don't know, it's just negative all the time, just attitude.

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: Here is your math task. You need to graph that problem.

STUDENT: I need help with this.

TUTOR: You gotta like, graph that problem with there.

STUDENT: You're just telling me what I need to do, not how to do it.

TUTOR: You just need to complete the work.

STUDENT: [voice sounds angry] You aren't really teaching me anything useful! I don't know, it's just negative all the time, just attitude.

context.social_setting: Interacting with a teacher when asking for help

challenging_behavior_trigger.condition: when they would ask for help they would say you gotta like, graph that problem with there and just tell them what they need to do not how to do it
related_quotes: you gotta like, graph that problem with there and just tell them what they need to do not how to do it

behavior_patterns.condition: When interacting with a teacher with a negative attitude
behavior_patterns.observable_behavior: states he did not learn much from them
behavior_patterns.internal_state: generally seemed angry with school and felt that his teachers had mistreated him
